title: cosmic-goo plugin
description: A cosmic-goo plugin TOML file: a flat document contributing any combination of types, sources, verbs, adverbs, channels, sigils, aliases, and dispatch rules. See doc/plugin-authoring.md. Item objects allow extra keys (verbs in particular carry custom {verb.*} template vars); the top level does not, so a singular-vs-plural typo like [[verb]] is caught.

tier.description: Plugin layer (discovery/priority): core (portable), cosmic (COSMIC desktop), desktop (generic Linux desktop).

verb_defaults.description: Per-verb default settings keyed by verb name, e.g. critique = { model = "haiku" } (consumed by the verbs that read them, e.g. claude-routing).

detectors.description: Content detectors ("what is this?"). Declared+validated, but the cmd runner is still in progress — see design/detection.md.

checkers.description: Content checkers ("is this usable as type X?"). Only the built-in json checker executes today — see design/detection.md.

mimePattern.description: A MIME type or glob (e.g. text/plain, text/*, application/vnd.tool.thing). Matching is subtype-aware (glob + structured-suffix + declared is_a).

name.description: The MIME type, conventionally application/vnd.<tool>.<subtype> for vendor handle types.

kind.description: handle = something to find; content = bytes (rarely declared); surface = a presentation target.

is_a.description: Supertype(s) — the subtype lattice walks these transitively (a DAG; cycles guarded).

extensions.description: File extensions mapped to this type (usually populated by the OS MIME-DB importer, not by hand).

prefix.description: Short distinctive prefix for :prefix addressing and prefix/rest inference (lowercase, 2-5 chars; avoid common English words).

emits.description: The primary type this source's items carry.

list_cmd.description: Shell command producing a JSON array of {id, title, subtitle?, metadata?} on stdout. id must be stable.

preview_cmd.description: Optional command to preview an item ({subject.*} substituted).

enumerate.description: Whether the source is bulk-listed (compose picker, tab completion). Default true; set false for slow/huge/noisy sources (still reachable on demand via :prefix:query).

inferable.description: Whether the source participates in bare-name entity inference. Defaults to (enumerate != false).

watch.description: File paths whose mtime gates this source's entity-cache freshness; the source is cached only if this is set (the cache is never stale). ~ is expanded.

cache_ttl.description: DEPRECATED — superseded by the watch/mtime cache model; ignored by the current engine.

verb.description: A named action. Extra keys are allowed: a verb's custom fields (e.g. fabric_pattern) are available in templates as {verb.<key>}.

name.description: Verb name (the subcommand). May be polymorphic — multiple impls with different accepts coexist.

accepts.description: MIME patterns this verb applies to (subtype-aware).

cmd.description: Command template, run via bash -c. {subject.*}/{object.*}/{verb.*}/{adverbs.*} are substituted (use |q to shell-quote).

prompt.description: Prompt template for adverb-routed verbs (the selector adverb supplies the cmd that consumes {verb.prompt}).

description.description: Shown by goo describe / goo what.

uses_adverbs.description: Selector/fill adverbs this verb opts into and resolves at run time.

default_for.description: The type(s) this verb is the default action for (a single type or an array of types).

valid_when.description: jq boolean predicate over the subject JSON; the verb applies only when truthy. Absent = always.

destructive.description: Marks an irreversible action ([!!] chip; subsumes confirm).

emits.description: The type the verb's result carries (for negotiation/routing).

kind.description: e.g. "present" — the subject IS the result and delivery is the engine's job.

usage.description: Channel names that can carry out this verb (the instrument slot; the planner / --using picks one).

object_type.description: For two-step verbs: the type of the second positional, available as {object.*}.

object_source.description: Named source (by name or prefix) supplying object candidates.

object_list_cmd.description: Shell snippet emitting object candidates as JSON ({subject.*} substituted first).

object_valid_when.description: jq predicate pruning the object pool ({subject.*} available, candidate is .).

adverb.description: Modifies how a verb runs. Must declare scope via exactly one of applies_to / applies_to_verbs.

kind.description: selector = pick among values; fill = free-form value (available as {adverbs.<name>}).

applies_to.description: Offered for any verb accepting these types.

applies_to_verbs.description: Offered only for these named verbs.

values_cmd.description: Shell command emitting extra completion candidates (one per line), merged after the static values. For live discovery, e.g. the model adverb curling woollama's /v1/models.

values.description: Selector alternatives, one table per value: [adverbs.values.NAME].

template.description: The cmd fragment this value contributes (consumes {verb.prompt} etc.).

template_var.description: Named vars injected into the verb's templates (e.g. {depth_prefix}).

channel.description: A type->type conversion edge (and optionally a verb instrument).

emits.description: The single CONCRETE type it produces — never a glob.

cost.description: Route-cost tier; the planner minimizes total cost (lossy/network are flagged in --explain).

tool.description: A PATH binary the cmd needs; the planner routes around it if absent, or 415s with install: <tool>.

requires.description: Environment capabilities that gate it, e.g. ["display"].

cmd.description: Conversion command; reads {in.path}, writes result to stdout.

provider.description: A dynamic verb provider: at verb-listing time, run list_cmd to enumerate verbs for any subject whose type matches for_type, synthesizing one verb per emitted {name, description}. Used to surface an external tool's command registry (e.g. blq) as goo verbs on a subject like :cwd. See doc/design/dynamic-verb-providers.md.

name.description: Provider id (unique; later plugins override by name).

for_type.description: The subject type these dynamic verbs attach to (subtype-aware). Keep it specific (e.g. application/vnd.goo.cwd), not a glob — every matching subject pays a list_cmd exec when its verbs are listed.

list_cmd.description: Shell command emitting a JSON array of verb stubs: [{"name": "...", "description": "..."}, …]. Runs in the user's cwd. Non-zero exit or non-JSON yields no verbs (graceful) — it must never break verb listing.

run.description: Command template for a chosen dynamic verb; the synthesized verb's cmd. {verb.name} (and {subject.*}) substitute at run time, e.g. "blq run {verb.name}".

confirm.description: Mark synthesized verbs as needing confirmation (default false).

sigil.description: A single leading character that expands into a goo:// address.

char.description: The single trigger character (not : + . / ~ or an alphanumeric — those are reserved/native).

alias.description: Abbreviates a whole invocation (verb + adverbs and/or a baked-in subject).

name.description: The leading word; must not shadow a subcommand. Sharing a verb name is allowed (the alias wins) but warned.

expands.description: Shell-tokenized tokens prepended at the verb position; re-dispatched (may chain, depth-guarded).

dispatch.description: A content-dispatch rule: classify raw text by a regex and route it to a verb (goo dispatch).

matches.description: Bash ERE; capture groups -> ${1}, ${2}, … (${0} = whole match). Use POSIX classes, not Perl shorthands.

type.description: Type assigned to the resulting subject.

set.description: Subject overrides deep-merged over { type, text: <input> } (e.g. rewrite text, add metadata).

adverbs.description: Optional adverb seed for the routed verb.
